what.problem_object: Exceptions panel approvals data refresh model
what.change_target: Shift from timer-driven refresh to bounded event-driven updates for approvals

mechanism: Subscribe to relevant realtime events for table_fill and table_credit
Patch or invalidate approvals query cache on matching events
Reduce or remove 15-second approvals polling after verification

where.backend_boundary: Supabase Realtime for relevant table events

when.interaction_moment: while Exceptions panel is open and operator is monitoring approvals

why.business_reason: Reduce operator distraction and improve trust in panel state by updating on meaningful events rather than arbitrary timer refresh
why.pilot_reason: Concrete user-facing pain with narrow blast radius and preexisting event concepts
why.rejection_reason_for_broader_scope: Platform-wide realtime investment is not justified by this single pain point yet

operator_problem.summary: Approvals polling every 15 seconds causes unnecessary panel churn without realtime support for the underlying fill/credit events.

containment_loop.entry_condition: Exceptions panel open; approvals state visible
containment_loop.exit_condition: Panel reflects new approvals state with less visual churn

required_outcomes.name: event_driven_approvals_updates
required_outcomes.description: Requested/completed fill and credit changes reach the Approvals tab without primary reliance on 15-second polling

required_outcomes.name: reduced_visual_churn
required_outcomes.description: The panel no longer re-renders on timer cadence when nothing changed

required_outcomes.name: pilot_bounded_implementation
required_outcomes.description: Implementation remains local to Exceptions panel approval behavior

required_outcomes.name: safe_cache_reconciliation
required_outcomes.description: Realtime integration reconciles via TanStack Query, using patch or invalidation appropriately

dependencies: Supabase Realtime access for relevant tables
Fill/credit status transitions can be mapped to event handlers
assumptions: Approvals tab is the highest operator pain point in this panel
Local implementation is acceptable for pilot scale
Reconnection resilience relies on polling fallback rather than ADR-004 §5 reconnection stack; deferred with canonical infrastructure

adjacent_rejected_ideas.reason: Too broad for the immediate pain and pilot scope

adjacent_rejected_ideas.idea: Keep polling and smooth the UI only
adjacent_rejected_ideas.reason: Symptom management without fixing event latency model

adjacent_rejected_ideas.idea: Convert entire panel to realtime in one pass
adjacent_rejected_ideas.reason: Unnecessary blast radius for first corrective slice

expand_if: multiple surfaces need near-identical local wiring
event bursts create cache thrash requiring batching
other widgets show comparable polling-fatigue cost

governance.scope_authority: Only pilot-bounded realtime wiring for Exceptions panel approvals path is authorized

source_context: User-provided diagnosis of Exceptions panel polling fatigue
Referenced canon gap: ADR-004 / event catalog not fully implemented
traceability.implementation_intent: Use the smallest viable realtime slice that materially reduces operator-facing churn
